title: ixeris taiwaniana
intro: Ixeris taiwaniana, also known as the Taiwan dandelion, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its roots, leaves, and flowers, which contain compounds like sesquiterpenes and triterpenoids that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Ixeris taiwaniana include its potential to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its ability to support liver and kidney function. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
